phases.title: Phase 1: Foundation
phases.subtitle: Days 1–7 — Set up your platform and tools

items.text: Start Kajabi free trial (30 days) at kajabi.com

subtasks.text: Go to kajabi.com and click 'Start Free Trial' — you get 30 days free, no credit card required initially
Enter your name, email (use ann@yogaismedicine.com), and create a password
Select your business type when prompted — choose 'Courses & Coaching' or the closest option
You will land on your Kajabi Dashboard — this is your home base. The left sidebar contains: Website, Products, Marketing, People, Analytics, and Settings.

PLAN NOTE: Kajabi is $179/month (Basic plan) after your trial. This replaces Unbounce (~$99-149/month) and adds course hosting. Keep Mailchimp for now so Manus can continue sending your email campaigns.

items.text: Complete Kajabi onboarding wizard

subtasks.text: ENTER YOUR SITE TITLE (Business Name): From your Dashboard → click Settings in the left sidebar → click Site Details → find the Title field → type 'Yoga Is Medicine' → click Save
SET YOUR SITE LANGUAGE & CURRENCY: Still in Settings → Site Details → scroll down to find Language and Currency fields → set Language to English (United States) and Currency to USD → click Save
SET YOUR HOMEPAGE: Still in Settings → Site Details → scroll to 'Homepage' → select which page you want visitors to land on first (you can change this later once your pages are built) → click Save
ADD YOUR LOGO: From Dashboard → Settings → Branding → click the Logo field → click 'Upload a new file' → select your Yoga Is Medicine logo file from your computer → click Save

SET YOUR BRAND COLORS: Still in Settings → Branding → scroll to Colors section → click the Brand color block → enter hex code #52796F → click the Accent color block → enter #354F52 → click Save. Note: Global Color Palette lets you add additional brand colors.

SET YOUR FONTS: Still in Settings → Branding → scroll to Typography → click Heading Font dropdown → choose a serif font (Cormorant Garamond is the closest available to your Adobe Mendl Sans Dusk) → click Paragraph Font dropdown → choose DM Sans or Lato → click Save
ADD SOCIAL MEDIA LINKS: Still in Settings → Branding → scroll to Social Media Links → enter your Instagram URL and Facebook URL → click Save. These will appear in your site footer automatically.

SET UP EMAIL SENDER NAME: From Dashboard → Settings → scroll down the left menu to find 'Marketing Settings' or 'Email Settings' → set From Name to 'Ann Chrapkiewicz' and From Email to ann@yogaismedicine.com → set Reply-To to ann@yogaismedicine.com → click Save

CONNECT STRIPE (Payments): From Dashboard → Settings → click 'Integrations & Webhooks' in the left menu → scroll to 'Third-party payment provider' → click 'View third-party payment provider' → click 'Connect' → log in with your existing Stripe account or create a new one → follow the on-screen prompts → click Submit when done. You will be redirected back to Kajabi when complete.

items.text: Set up global branding (fonts and colors) in Kajabi Style Guide

subtasks.text: From your Dashboard → click Settings in the left sidebar → click Branding
FAVICON: Click the Favicon field → upload a small square version of your logo (recommended: 32x32 or 64x64 pixels, PNG format). This appears in browser tabs.
LOGO: Click the Logo field → upload your full Yoga Is Medicine logo. This appears in your site header and email templates.
TYPOGRAPHY — Heading Font: Click the Heading Font dropdown → choose a serif font. Cormorant Garamond is the closest available to your Adobe Mendl Sans Dusk. Playfair Display is another good option.
TYPOGRAPHY — Paragraph Font: Click the Paragraph Font dropdown → choose DM Sans or Lato for clean, readable body text.

COLORS — Brand: Click the Brand color block → enter hex code #52796F (your medium teal). Click the Accent color block → enter #354F52 (your dark teal). Use the Global Color Palette to add your full brand palette: #CAD2C5, #84A89C, #52796F, #354F52, #2F3E46
SOCIAL MEDIA LINKS: Scroll down to Social Media Links → enter your Instagram and Facebook URLs. These display in your site footer automatically.

PREVIEW & SAVE: Use the real-time preview on the right to see how your changes look before saving. When satisfied, click Save. Note: Pages and emails you have already customized will NOT automatically update — you will need to sync them individually using the globe icon (Style Guide sync) within each page or email editor.

items.text: Add logo to email templates in Kajabi

subtasks.text: Your logo is added to email templates automatically once you set it in Settings → Branding (see previous step). Verify this by going to Dashboard → Marketing → Email Campaigns → click 'New Email' → check that your logo appears in the header.
If the logo does not appear automatically: From Dashboard → Settings → Branding → confirm your logo is uploaded → click Save → return to your email template and refresh.

To customize email template layout: Dashboard → Settings → scroll to find 'Email Templates' if available on your plan → click to edit the default template → adjust header, footer, and color settings to match your brand.
Set your email footer text: In your email template settings, add your physical mailing address (required by CAN-SPAM law) — use your business address or a PO Box.
Click Save — all automated Kajabi emails (purchase confirmations, course access, etc.) will now show your logo and brand colors.

items.text: Connect Kajabi to Mailchimp so new subscribers and buyers sync automatically

subtasks.text: STEP 1 — Enable Mailchimp integration: Dashboard → Settings → click 'Third Party Integrations' in the left menu → find Mailchimp in the list → toggle it ON → a field will appear asking for your Mailchimp API key → paste: [YOUR-MAILCHIMP-API-KEY] → click Save

STEP 2 — Get your Mailchimp Audience ID: Log into Mailchimp (mailchimp.com) → click Audience in the top menu → click All Contacts → click Settings tab → click 'Audience name and defaults' → copy the Audience ID shown (a short alphanumeric code like 'abc123def4'). Paste it in the Notes field of this checklist item.

STEP 3 — Connect your opt-in form: Dashboard → Marketing → Forms → open your lead magnet opt-in form → scroll to 'Integrations' or 'After Submit' settings → check 'Send the contact to a third party email provider' → select Mailchimp → paste your Audience ID → click Save

STEP 4 — Connect your course offer: Dashboard → Products → your mini-course → click Settings → find 'After Purchase' or 'Integrations' → check 'Send to third party email provider' → select Mailchimp → paste Audience ID → click Save
STEP 5 — Test: Submit a test entry on your Kajabi opt-in page → check your Mailchimp Audience within 5 minutes to confirm the contact appeared with the correct tags
STEP 6 — Tell Manus once this is live: I will set up the Mailchimp automation rules so buyers are tagged 'kajabi-customer' and lead magnet downloaders are tagged 'lead-magnet' automatically

items.text: Set up Mailchimp tags for Kajabi buyers and lead magnet subscribers

subtasks.text: This step is handled by Manus — tell me when your Kajabi-Mailchimp connection is live and I will create the tags and automation rules via the Mailchimp API
Tags I will create: 'kajabi-customer', 'lead-magnet-downloaded', 'mini-course-buyer', 'email-sequence-active'
I will also set up a webhook so any new Kajabi purchase automatically updates the subscriber's Mailchimp tags within minutes

items.text: Create or connect your Stripe account

subtasks.text: Go to stripe.com — if you have an account, log in; if not, click 'Start now' and create one with ann@yogaismedicine.com
Complete Stripe's identity verification: provide your legal name, date of birth, address, and SSN (last 4 digits) — this is required to receive payouts
Add your bank account for payouts: Stripe Dashboard → Settings → Bank accounts and scheduling → Add bank account
Set payout schedule: Stripe Dashboard → Settings → Bank accounts and scheduling → change to 'Daily' or 'Weekly' based on your preference
Note your Stripe account email — you will need it when connecting to Kajabi in the next step

items.text: Connect Stripe to Kajabi

subtasks.text: From Kajabi Dashboard → click Settings in the left sidebar → click 'Integrations & Webhooks'
Scroll down to find the 'Third-party payment provider' section → click 'View third-party payment provider'
Click 'Connect' → you will be redirected to a Stripe authorization page

If you have an existing Stripe account: click 'Log in to Stripe' → enter your Stripe email and password → select your account → click Connect. Note: if your Stripe account is already connected to another Kajabi site, you will need to create a new Stripe account.
If creating a new Stripe account: click 'Create Stripe account' → enter your email and password → click Continue → follow the on-screen identity verification steps
After connecting, you will be redirected back to Kajabi's Payments page — confirm it shows 'Connected' next to Stripe. You can now accept payments for your courses and offers.

items.text: Define your mini-course title and core promise

subtasks.text: Working title: 'Internal Literacy: A 4-Week Practice' — confirm or refine this
Write your one-sentence promise: 'In 4 weeks, you will learn to read your body from the inside out — so your practice becomes a conversation, not a performance'
Define your ideal student: someone who has been practicing yoga for 1–5 years and feels like they're doing the poses correctly but not feeling them deeply
Set your price point: $47 for the mini-course (confirm this is right for your audience)
Write this all down in the Notes field above so you can reference it throughout the build

items.text: Outline the 4 modules of your mini-course

subtasks.text: Module 1: What is Internal Literacy? — the concept, why it matters, what changes when you have it
Module 2: The Breath as Your First Teacher — pranayama as internal sensing, not performance
Module 3: Reading a Posture from the Inside — pick 3–4 key postures (e.g., Standing Bow, Triangle, Savasana) and teach the internal experience of each
Module 4: Building a Daily Internal Practice — how to carry this into every class and every day
Each module: 2–3 short videos (10–20 min each), one reflection prompt, one practice assignment

items.text: Record Module 1 videos using your existing Vimeo setup

subtasks.text: Set up your recording space: good natural light, clean background, your usual teaching environment
Record Video 1 (10–15 min): Introduction to Internal Literacy — what it is, your story, why you teach it
Record Video 2 (10–15 min): The difference between external and internal cues — demonstrate with one posture
Record Video 3 (5–10 min): Your invitation to the student — what to expect, how to approach the course
Upload to Vimeo → set privacy to 'Only people with the private link' → copy the embed links for Kajabi

phases.title: Phase 2: Content Creation
phases.subtitle: Days 8–18 — Build your lead magnet and course

items.text: Write the free guide: '5 Postures That Changed Everything'

subtasks.text: Open a new document in Google Docs or Word
Title page: '5 Postures That Changed Everything — An Internal Literacy Guide by Ann Chrapkiewicz'
Introduction (1 page): your story — why these 5 postures, what internal literacy means, what the reader will gain
For each of the 5 postures (1–2 pages each): posture name, external cue vs. internal experience, what to feel for, one reflection question
Suggested postures: Standing Bow, Triangle, Camel, Savasana, Child's Pose — adjust to your teaching
Closing page: brief bio, website URL, invitation to your mini-course, and a warm sign-off in your voice
Tell Manus (me) when the text is written — I will help you refine the language to match your teaching voice

items.text: Design the PDF guide using Adobe Express or Adobe InDesign

subtasks.text: ADOBE EXPRESS (recommended for speed): Go to express.adobe.com → click 'Create new' → choose 'Document' or 'PDF' → search templates for 'wellness guide' or 'yoga guide'
ADOBE INDESIGN (recommended for quality): Open InDesign → File → New Document → set size to Letter (8.5x11) or A4 → set up a 2-column layout with your brand colors
Apply your brand colors: #CAD2C5 (background), #52796F (headings), #2F3E46 (body text), #84A89C (accents)
Use Mendl Sans Dusk for headings (you have Adobe, so it's available in your font library) and a clean serif for body text
Add your Colorado mountain portrait photo on the cover or introduction page
Export as PDF: File → Export → Adobe PDF (Print) → click Export — save as 'YIM-5-Postures-Guide.pdf'
Upload the PDF to Kajabi: Dashboard → Products → click '+' → choose 'Download' → upload your PDF → set price to Free → publish

items.text: Create the opt-in landing page in Kajabi

subtasks.text: From Kajabi Dashboard → click 'Marketing' in the left sidebar → click 'Landing Pages'
Click 'New Landing Page' → choose a simple opt-in template
Headline: 'Download Your Free Guide: 5 Postures That Changed Everything'
Subheadline: 'A free Internal Literacy guide from Ann Chrapkiewicz — learn to feel your practice from the inside out'
Form fields: First Name + Email only (keep it simple)
Button text: 'Send Me the Free Guide'
Add your Colorado mountain portrait or a yoga image as the page background or sidebar image
Connect the form to Mailchimp: check 'Send to third party email provider' → select Mailchimp → paste your Audience ID → click Save
Publish the page → copy the URL → this is the link you will share in all your emails and social posts

items.text: Create a Thank You page in Kajabi (post opt-in)

subtasks.text: From Kajabi Dashboard → Marketing → Landing Pages → New Landing Page → choose 'Thank You' template
Headline: 'Your guide is on its way!'
Body: 'Check your inbox for an email from ann@yogaismedicine.com — your free guide is attached. While you wait, here is something I want you to know...'
Add a brief personal note in your voice — 2–3 sentences about what you hope they discover in the guide
Optional: add a soft mention of your mini-course — 'If you want to go deeper, I am building something just for you...'
Publish the page → go back to your opt-in form settings → set the redirect URL to this Thank You page URL

items.text: Create your mini-course product in Kajabi

subtasks.text: From Kajabi Dashboard → click 'Products' in the left sidebar → click the '+' button → select 'Mini Course'
Title: 'Internal Literacy: A 4-Week Practice' (or your confirmed title)
Description: write 2–3 paragraphs describing what students will learn, feel, and experience — use your teaching voice
Thumbnail image: upload a photo of yourself teaching or your Colorado mountain portrait — this appears in your course library
Click 'Save' — your course shell is now created

items.text: Build Module 1 in Kajabi

subtasks.text: Inside your course → click 'Add Section' → name it 'Module 1: What is Internal Literacy?'
Click 'Add Post' → name it 'Welcome + Introduction' → click the video icon → paste your Vimeo embed link for Video 1
Add a text block below the video with your reflection prompt for Module 1
Repeat for Video 2 and Video 3 — each gets its own Post within Module 1
Click 'Save' after each post — you can preview how it looks by clicking the eye icon

items.text: Set pricing and create your Offer in Kajabi

subtasks.text: From Dashboard → Products → your course → click 'Offers' tab → click 'New Offer'
Offer name: 'Internal Literacy Mini-Course — $47'
Price: $47 one-time payment
Select which products are included: check your mini-course
After purchase settings: check 'Send to third party email provider' → select Mailchimp → paste Audience ID → this tags buyers in Mailchimp automatically
Click Save → click Publish → copy the checkout URL — this is your sales link

items.text: Create your sales/checkout page in Kajabi

subtasks.text: From Dashboard → Marketing → Landing Pages → New Landing Page → choose a Sales Page template
Headline: 'Internal Literacy: A 4-Week Practice' — subtitle: 'Learn to feel your yoga from the inside out'
Add sections: What you will learn, Who this is for, What is inside (module breakdown), About Ann (short bio + photo), Price + CTA button
CTA button text: 'Begin Your Practice — $47' → link it to your Kajabi Offer checkout URL
Apply your brand colors and upload your portrait photo
Publish the page → copy the URL → this is what you will link to in your sales emails

items.text: Create social media graphics for the lead magnet launch using Adobe Express

subtasks.text: Go to express.adobe.com → click 'Create new' → choose 'Social Media Post'
Select Instagram Post size (1080x1080) → search for a clean, minimal template
Apply brand colors: background #CAD2C5, text #2F3E46, accent #52796F
Text: 'Free Guide: 5 Postures That Changed Everything — Download at [your link]'
Use Mendl Sans Dusk for the headline (available in your Adobe fonts library)
Create 3 variations: one with your photo, one text-only, one with a yoga pose image
Download as PNG → save to your phone and computer for posting

items.text: Create email header image for Mailchimp campaigns using Adobe Express

subtasks.text: Go to express.adobe.com → Create new → choose 'Email Header' (600x200px is standard for Mailchimp)
Design: your logo on the left, 'Yoga Is Medicine' text on the right, brand color background #CAD2C5
Keep it simple and clean — this appears at the top of every email I send for you
Download as PNG → send the file to Manus (me) and I will upload it to your Mailchimp template

items.text: Define your three product tiers in Kajabi
subtasks: Tier 1 (Free): Lead magnet landing page — PDF guide + 1 free audio class
Tier 2 ($47 one-time): 'Original Hot Yoga: Your First 30 Days' — 4-week drip mini-course
Tier 3 ($29–$39/mo): 'The Full Practice Library' — static library + monthly new content
In Kajabi: go to Products → Create New Product → select 'Course' for Tiers 1 and 2, 'Membership' for Tier 3
Name Tier 2 course: 'Original Hot Yoga: Your First 30 Days'
Name Tier 3 membership: 'The Full Practice Library'

items.text: Set up the Foundations Mini-Course (Tier 2 — $47 drip course)
subtasks: In Kajabi: Products → select your Tier 2 course → go to Content
Create 4 modules: Week 1 (Your First Practice), Week 2 (Understanding the Postures), Week 3 (Building Your Practice), Week 4 (Going Deeper)
Week 1: Upload 1 introductory 90-min audio class + add a short welcome text post
Week 2: Upload 2 posture tutorial recordings + add written posture guide as a PDF or text post
Week 3: Upload 1 shorter class (60 or 75 min) + add breathing guidance audio
Week 4: Upload 1 therapeutic sequence + add 'What's Next' text post inviting upgrade to Full Library
Set drip schedule: Settings → Drip → Week 1 available immediately, Week 2 unlocks Day 8, Week 3 Day 15, Week 4 Day 22
Set price: Products → Offers → Create Offer → $47 one-time payment
Add upsell: after Week 4 completion, trigger automated email inviting upgrade to Full Library Membership

items.text: Set up the Full Library Membership (Tier 3 — $29–$39/month)
subtasks: In Kajabi: Products → select your Membership product → go to Content

Create these library categories as separate sections: '90-Minute Audio Classes', '60 & 75-Minute Audio Classes', 'Therapeutic Audio Sequences', 'Video Classes', 'Posture Tutorials', 'Yoga Homework & Resources'
Set price: Products → Offers → Create Offer → choose Monthly Recurring → set price at $29 or $39/month
Enable 'Founding Member' offer: create a second offer at $19–$24/month available only during launch window
Set up monthly drip slot: create a 'Monthly New Content' section — this is where you upload each month's new class on the 1st
Set up 'Ann's Monthly Note' as a recurring post template in the Monthly New Content section

items.text: Configure the upsell funnel from mini-course to membership
subtasks: In Kajabi: Automations → Create Automation
Trigger: 'Course completed' (Tier 2 mini-course)
Action 1: Send email — subject 'You did it — here's your alumni offer' with link to Founding Member membership offer
Action 2: Apply tag 'mini-course-graduate' to the contact
Action 3: Wait 3 days → send follow-up email if they have not yet joined the membership
Set the Founding Member offer to expire 7 days after the email is sent (use Kajabi's offer expiry setting)

items.text: ✅ COMPLETE: Email 1 drafted, photos added, test sent to ann@yogaismedicine.com
Review the test email in your inbox — confirm photos display correctly and links work

subtasks.text: Open your inbox at ann@yogaismedicine.com
Find the test email from Mailchimp — subject line starts with '[Test]'
Check: do both photos display? Is the text readable? Do any links work?
Check on your phone too — confirm it looks good on mobile
If anything looks wrong, tell Manus (me) and I will fix it in Mailchimp before you send

items.text: Log into Mailchimp → Campaigns → find 'Email 1 — Re-Engagement' → click Send

subtasks.text: Go to mailchimp.com → log in with ann.chrapkiewicz@gmail.com
Click 'Campaigns' in the top navigation
Find 'Email 1 — Re-Engagement (Restored Dreams)' in your campaign list
Click the dropdown arrow next to it → click 'Schedule' or 'Send'
Best send time: Tuesday or Wednesday between 9–11am your local time
Confirm your audience is set to your full list (2,329 subscribers) → click 'Send Now' or schedule it

items.text: After sending: monitor open rate (goal: 25%+) and reply rate (goal: 5+ replies)

subtasks.text: 24 hours after sending: Mailchimp → Campaigns → click on Email 1 → click 'View Report'
Check: Open Rate (good = 25%+), Click Rate (good = 2%+), Unsubscribes (normal = under 0.5%)
Read any replies that come to ann@yogaismedicine.com — these are gold. Reply personally to each one
Tell Manus (me) the open rate and any notable replies — I will use this to refine Email 2

items.text: Subject line: 'The thing I've been trying to teach for 20 years has a name'

subtasks.text: This email is ready to be built in Mailchimp — tell Manus (me) when you are ready and I will create it
Send timing: 3–5 days after Email 1
Goal: introduce Internal Literacy as a concept, build curiosity, invite replies

items.text: Opening: Reference replies from Email 1 — 'So many of you wrote back, and what you said stopped me in my tracks'
Body: Introduce the concept of Internal Literacy — what it means, why it changes everything
Include one specific example: a posture (e.g., Standing Bow) and what internal literacy reveals in it
Photo: Use the Colorado mountain portrait (close-up) — warm, thoughtful, personal
Soft CTA: 'Reply and tell me — what posture do you most want to understand from the inside?'
P.S.: Mention you're working on a guide — plant the seed for the lead magnet
Subject line: 'I made you something (it's free)'

subtasks.text: Tell Manus (me) when your lead magnet PDF is ready — I will build this email in Mailchimp for you
Send timing: 3–4 days after Email 2
This email goes to your full list of 2,329 — it is your biggest opportunity to grow your Kajabi opt-in list

items.text: Body: Announce the free guide — '5 Postures That Changed Everything'
Explain what's inside: what they'll learn, what they'll feel, why it matters
Clear CTA button: 'Download Your Free Guide'
Link to your Kajabi opt-in landing page
Email 4 (Day 1 after download): 'Did you get a chance to try it?' — check in, encourage action

subtasks.text: This is an automated email set up in Kajabi — triggered when someone downloads the lead magnet
Tell Manus (me) when your Kajabi is set up — I will write the copy and guide you through setting up the automation

items.text: Email 5 (Day 3): Deep dive on one concept from the guide — build authority
Email 6 (Day 5): Student transformation story — someone whose practice changed
Email 7 (Day 7): Soft introduction to the mini-course — 'If you want to go deeper...'
Set these 4 emails up as an automated sequence in Kajabi (triggered by lead magnet download)

subtasks.text: From Kajabi Dashboard → Marketing → Automations → New Automation
Trigger: 'Form submitted' → select your lead magnet opt-in form
Add Action: 'Send Email' → create Email 4 content → set delay to 1 day
Add Action: 'Send Email' → Email 5 → delay 3 days
Add Action: 'Send Email' → Email 6 → delay 5 days
Add Action: 'Send Email' → Email 7 → delay 7 days
Click Publish → your nurture sequence is now live and automatic

items.text: Email 8: Launch announcement — 'It's here. Internal Literacy: 4 Weeks to a Deeper Practice'

subtasks.text: Tell Manus (me) your course launch date — I will build and schedule this email in Mailchimp

items.text: Email 9 (Day 2): What's inside — module breakdown, video count, what they will feel
Email 10 (Day 4): FAQ email — answer the 5 most common questions and objections
Email 11 (Day 6): Testimonial email — student story, before/after, specific transformation
Email 12 (Day 7): Last chance — cart closes tonight, personal note from Ann
Add a 'Free Guide' banner or popup to yogaismedicine.com linking to your Kajabi opt-in page

subtasks.text: Tell Manus (me) what website platform yogaismedicine.com is built on (WordPress, Squarespace, Wix, etc.)
Once confirmed, I will write the banner copy and provide the embed code or instructions for your specific platform
Banner copy suggestion: 'Free Guide: 5 Postures That Changed Everything → Download Now'

items.text: Add an 'Online Courses' page or nav item to your website — link to your Kajabi sales page

subtasks.text: In your website editor, add 'Courses' or 'Online Learning' to your main navigation
Link it to your Kajabi sales page URL
Add a brief description: 'Deepen your practice with Ann's online courses — available anywhere, anytime'

items.text: Update your About page bio to mention your online offerings
Add the Colorado mountain portrait as your new bio photo on the website
Ensure your website footer has links to: Free Guide, Mini-Course, Email Signup
Instagram: announce the free guide with a Reel — film yourself explaining what Internal Literacy is

subtasks.text: Script (30–60 seconds): 'I've been teaching yoga for 20 years and there is one thing I wish someone had told me on day one — [pause] — it's called Internal Literacy. Here is what it means and why it changes everything...'
End with: 'I made a free guide. Link in bio.'
Film in good light, facing the camera, in your teaching space or outdoors
Caption: write 3–5 sentences expanding on the concept + call to action + hashtags
Tell Manus (me) when you want to post — I can write the full caption for you in your voice

items.text: Instagram bio: update link to your Kajabi opt-in page

subtasks.text: Go to Instagram → Edit Profile → Website → paste your Kajabi opt-in page URL
Update bio text to include: 'Free guide → link below' or 'Download my free Internal Literacy guide → link in bio'

items.text: Facebook: share the same Reel + a longer written post about your teaching philosophy
YouTube: record a 10-minute 'Introduction to Internal Literacy' video as a free preview
Pin the free guide announcement to the top of your Instagram and Facebook profiles
Kajabi pipeline: build a 3-step pipeline — Opt-in page → Thank you page → Nurture sequence

subtasks.text: From Kajabi Dashboard → Marketing → Pipelines → New Pipeline → choose 'Lead Magnet' template
Step 1: connect your existing opt-in landing page
Step 2: connect your Thank You page
Step 3: connect your email automation sequence
Click Publish → your full lead magnet funnel is now live and automated

items.text: Kajabi-Mailchimp sync: new Kajabi customer → tag 'kajabi-customer' in Mailchimp automatically

subtasks.text: This is handled by Manus (me) via the Mailchimp API — tell me when your Kajabi offer is live and I will set up the tagging automation
What will happen automatically: new buyer → tagged 'kajabi-customer' in Mailchimp → removed from 'prospect' segment → added to 'customer' segment

items.text: Kajabi: set up affiliate program for students who want to refer others

subtasks.text: From Kajabi Dashboard → Settings → Affiliates → Enable Affiliates
Set commission rate: 20–30% is standard for digital courses
Create an affiliate sign-up page — Kajabi generates this automatically
Invite your most engaged students to become affiliates after your first launch

items.text: Create a 'Welcome' automated email for new Mailchimp subscribers

subtasks.text: Tell Manus (me) to build this — I will create it in Mailchimp as an automated welcome email
Triggered by: new subscriber joins your Mailchimp audience
Content: warm welcome, who you are, what they can expect, link to free guide

items.text: Set a monthly calendar reminder: review Kajabi analytics and email open rates

subtasks.text: Set a recurring reminder on the 1st of each month: 'Review Kajabi + Mailchimp analytics'
What to review: Kajabi — course completions, new purchases, pipeline conversions; Mailchimp — open rates, click rates, unsubscribes, list growth
Share the numbers with Manus (me) and I will help you interpret them and suggest next steps

items.text: Set a quarterly reminder: update the lead magnet and refresh email sequences
Month 1 goal: 50 lead magnet downloads, 5 mini-course sales ($235)
Month 3 goal: 200 downloads, 20 mini-course sales ($940), 2 signature course inquiries
Month 6 goal: 500 downloads, 50 mini-course sales ($2,350), 10 signature course students
Month 12 goal: 1,000+ downloads, 100 mini-course sales/year ($4,700), recurring revenue established
Track weekly: new subscribers, lead magnet downloads, course sales, email open rates
Celebrate every milestone — this is a practice, not a sprint. Each step forward matters.

phases.title: Phase 5: Class Library Migration
phases.description: Migrate your 60+ existing recordings from WordPress to Kajabi, organized for maximum discoverability and student experience.

items.text: Audit and categorize all 30+ audio recordings
subtasks: List all audio recordings currently in your WordPress Members Classroom
Sort into categories: 90-Minute Classes, 60–75 Minute Classes, Therapeutic Sequences, Intermediate/Enrichment, Special Classes
Note the file format for each (MP3 preferred for Kajabi audio uploads)
Identify any recordings that need re-recording or quality improvement before migration

items.text: Upload audio library to Kajabi
subtasks: In Kajabi: go to your Full Library Membership product → Content
For each category section, click 'Add Post' → select 'Audio' as the post type
Upload the MP3 file directly to Kajabi (Kajabi hosts audio natively — no Vimeo needed for audio)
Add a title, brief description (2–3 sentences about the class), and duration to each post
Recommended title format: '[Duration] — [Theme/Focus] — [Special notes if any]' e.g. '90 Min — Full Bikram Series — Recorded Live'
Upload in batches of 5–10 per session to avoid browser timeouts

items.text: Set up audio library navigation and search
subtasks: In Kajabi: Membership → Customize → ensure each category section has a clear title and description
Add a 'Start Here' post at the top of the library with a 200-word guide to navigating the library
Add a 'Recommended Sequences' post that suggests 3–4 learning paths (e.g., 'New to Bikram', 'Therapeutic Focus', 'Building Stamina')

items.text: Audit and categorize all 30+ video recordings
subtasks: List all video recordings currently in your WordPress Members Classroom and on Vimeo
Sort into categories: 90-Minute Full Classes, Shorter Classes (60–75 min), Five Tibetan Rites, Therapeutic Practices
Note which videos are already on Vimeo (you will embed these in Kajabi rather than re-uploading)
Note which videos need to be uploaded fresh to Vimeo first

items.text: Embed Vimeo videos into Kajabi membership
subtasks: For each video: go to Vimeo → open the video → click Share → copy the video URL
In Kajabi: Membership → Content → Add Post → select 'Video' → paste the Vimeo URL
Kajabi will automatically embed the Vimeo player — no need to re-upload the video file
Add title, description, and duration to each video post
Set Vimeo privacy to 'Only on sites I choose' and add learn.yogaismedicine.com as an allowed domain

items.text: Create video library navigation
subtasks: Organize video posts into the same category structure as the audio library for consistency
Add thumbnail images to video posts — use a still frame from the video or your brand-colored image
Add a 'Video vs. Audio: Which Should I Use?' post explaining the difference and when each is helpful

items.text: Notify existing WordPress members of the migration
subtasks: I will write and send this email via Mailchimp — just confirm the date you want to send it
Email subject: 'Your class library has a new home — here's how to access it'
Offer existing members a Founding Member rate ($19–$24/mo) as a loyalty reward
Include a step-by-step guide for creating their Kajabi account and accessing the library

items.text: Update WordPress Members Classroom redirect
subtasks: Log into WordPress admin → Pages → find 'Bikram Yoga Online Members' page
Replace the page content with a simple redirect message: 'Our class library has moved to our new Online Studio at learn.yogaismedicine.com'
Add a large button linking to learn.yogaismedicine.com
Keep the page live (do not delete it) so existing bookmarks still work

items.text: Add 'Online Studio' link to WordPress navigation
subtasks: Log into WordPress admin → Appearance → Menus
Add a new Custom Link menu item: Label = 'Online Studio', URL = https://learn.yogaismedicine.com
Place it in the main navigation menu, ideally between 'Resources' and 'Contact'
Consider adding a small badge or indicator like 'New' next to it for the first 3 months

items.text: Set up Kajabi–Mailchimp integration for automatic subscriber tagging
subtasks: In Kajabi: Settings → Integrations → find Mailchimp → click Connect
Enter your Mailchimp API key: (stored securely — ask me to retrieve it when needed)
Map Kajabi events to Mailchimp tags: Purchase of Tier 2 → tag 'mini-course-buyer'; Membership signup → tag 'library-member'; Cancellation → tag 'churned-member'
Test the integration: create a test purchase and verify the tag appears in Mailchimp within 5 minutes
I will handle all Mailchimp segmentation and email sending once this integration is active

items.text: Establish your monthly content release schedule
subtasks: Choose a release date: the 1st of each month is recommended for predictability
Your monthly contribution: upload 1 new class recording to Kajabi's 'Monthly New Content' section
My monthly contribution: write Ann's Monthly Note (200-word reflection) based on the theme of your new class — you dictate the theme to me
I will write and send the monthly member email via Mailchimp on the 1st of each month
Set a recurring reminder: on the 25th of each month, record or upload your new class so it's ready for the 1st

items.text: Create the first monthly content release
subtasks: Choose a theme for Month 1 (e.g., 'Returning to Your Practice', 'The Foundation Series', 'Spring Renewal')
Upload the Month 1 class to Kajabi: Membership → Monthly New Content → Add Post
Tell me the theme and any notes about the class — I will write Ann's Monthly Note
I will draft the Month 1 member email for your review before sending
